mechanism_narrative: Sustained increases in real retail sales (RSAFS) indicate strong consumer spending, which directly drives corporate revenue growth and supports higher equity valuations over a one-month horizon, producing positive S&P 500 returns.
failure_condition: Rolling 2-year Rank IC between RSAFS momentum and 20-day forward returns is non-positive, or the strategy based on RSAFS trend fails to achieve >55% win rate in out-of-sample periods.

mechanism_narrative: When the unemployment rate (UNRATE) rises above 4.5%, it signals labor market slack that reduces aggregate demand and corporate profit expectations, causing negative S&P 500 returns over the subsequent 20 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered periods (UNRATE > 4.5%), the average 20-day forward return is not significantly negative (one-sided t-test p > 0.10), or the hit rate for negative returns is below 40%.

mechanism_narrative: When monthly CPI inflation (CPIAUCSL) exceeds 0.2% month-over-month and the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS) remains below the current CPI year-over-year rate, real interest rates are negative, encouraging equity investment and producing positive S&P 500 returns over 20 trading days.
failure_condition: The interaction term (CPI MoM growth * FEDFUNDS spread) has rank IC <= 0 over a 3-year rolling window, or the long-short portfolio based on negative real rates yields Sharpe ratio below 0.5.

mechanism_narrative: In economic expansions (positive GDPC1 quarterly growth), rising industrial production (INDPRO) signals increasing output and future earnings, boosting S&P 500 returns. In recessions (negative GDPC1 growth), rising INDPRO reflects inventory buildup and weak demand, leading to negative returns. The direction depends on the GDPC1 growth regime.
regime_hint: economic regime (expansion vs recession based on GDPC1 growth sign)
failure_condition: The regime-switched signal fails to produce a significant difference in average forward returns between expansion and recession regimes (two-sample t-test p > 0.10), or the directional accuracy is below 50% in either regime.

mechanism_narrative: When housing starts (HOUST) exceed 1,500 thousand units annually, it indicates robust residential investment and consumer confidence, which typically precedes higher S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days as the economy expands.
failure_condition: In triggered periods, the median 20-day forward return is not positive, or the win rate for positive returns is below 50% after accounting for market-wide trends.

mechanism_narrative: Rising total nonfarm payroll employment (PAYEMS) indicates a strengthening labor market that boosts consumer income and spending, leading to higher corporate profits and positive S&P 500 returns over a one-week horizon.
failure_condition: The 5-day forward return following positive PAYEMS surprises (month-over-month change) has a negative average over any 1-year period, or the rank IC with payroll growth is below 0.02.

mechanism_narrative: A simultaneous decline in retail sales (RSAFS drop >1% month-over-month) and rise in unemployment (UNRATE increase >0.1 percentage point in the same month) signals a sudden consumer spending collapse and labor market weakening, which strongly predicts negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
candidate_trigger: RSAFS decline >1% MoM AND UNRATE increase >0.1 in same month
failure_condition: The joint trigger signal fails to produce a median negative 20-day forward return, or the strategy yields a Sharpe ratio less than 0.3 during known recessionary periods.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp increase in the federal funds rate (FEDFUNDS rising more than 50 basis points over a 3-month window) signals an aggressive monetary tightening cycle that raises discount rates and depresses equity valuations, leading to negative S&P 500 returns over the following 5 trading days.
failure_condition: In triggered instances, the average 5-day forward return is not negative, or more than 60% of these instances are followed by positive returns, contradicting the predicted direction.
